textByHash.0a8e7121a0c764cfebc7907b5892427564931ff1: It appears that they were trying to use the power of haemalchemy to modify both humans and vampyres.
textByHash.151061ca81987bb0860079cfed0ffe530c9d037c: Serafina? As in the Serafina who survived the Theatre of Blood?
textByHash.18d32cd6be7c7d781974812168b5289d60c8de79: I do. I'll stay here and keep working on this book. You head out and see if you can find out what Serafina discovered. Safalaan should be able to help you.
textByHash.1e9dc5eb7021fd4103ff9dd3bd6e494e4dcd5d6f: Indeed I am. It's not a quick process I'm afraid. Large parts of the book are written using the vampyric language which isn't easy to translate.
textByHash.2c8246f02273c05216b29ca8f62036afa5314d3a: That would actually make a lot of sense. We've always wondered how Serafina managed to survive all of the challenges in the Theatre.
textByHash.341016216d7f7c6dba5c6e288d528d24a18f0c02: This book implies that the vampyres immunity isn't perfect and that it can be overcome. It seems they were trying to improve this immunity.
textByHash.4177f56d9ae27f9b4e9a153121bba11d9a91f0a3: I haven't got that far yet I'm afraid.
textByHash.4ffeafbf6b0a4edb8492b3982a17cd89d2c90d8c: That's the more interesting one. As we're already aware, vampyres possess some form of immunity against our attacks.
textByHash.5f20572ec1d871d63ad76a577f0006dced376d0a: The book seems to document a lot of the experiments being performed in that lab you found. Interestingly, the experiments were performed on vampyres themselves, as well as humans.
textByHash.8605371282f911bdeb24009c836712c842c27d59: It looks like they had two reasons. First of all they were looking for a way to increase the amount of blood that can be harvested from a human.
textByHash.8656777f5c0a61115b094851adb2ab0dcc3e1647: As in nothing. It's just a load of rubbish. Why would Serafina have written this?
textByHash.87750789a8a2ac009a077d233c1cbd7fa89c64fb: Like I say, it's slow. My knowledge on the language only comes from things I've seen around the city so I'm having to make a lot of assumptions. I'm making progress though.
textByHash.9251d8d677625830dfca189bf2a43a6ff8c75f8e: But what if she never needed to? Maybe she took advantage of the vampyres weakness and forced them to let her leave?
textByHash.9aa969856b740b9740cb24bce72c3521646547eb: We need to find out what Serafina discovered about the vampyres. Talk to Safalaan, he should be able to help you.
textByHash.9f26c2e898e8acc392528e79c8d696fd5b880fc0: Unsurprisingly, the vampyres here have multiplied much faster than the humans. Perhaps there's no longer enough blood to go around.
textByHash.af74ecbf64f3bb129b548034766101ad47dc5fcb: It starts off as only partial in juveniles and juvinates but by the time they become vyres they are totally invulnerable.
textByHash.b2d47a9fb2f24a7104110e0e2d3d33711c693e65: There are some that believe there is a hidden power in blood. Haemalchemy is the act of modifying blood, both magically and scientifically, to harness this power.
textByHash.c538d206bd83dce02b97d9770d10c0e4d997ddbd: It seems that the vampyres are strong believers in the power of haemalchemy as they've been experimenting quite heavily.
textByHash.e4c7669907f8d45e946133ef352f29dfff7afeb5: Oh yes. It seems to be very old and they don't use it much anymore but it does exist. I suspect it comes in quite useful for things like this where they don't want humans prying into their work.
textByHash.ff2c459d7a507eb5a51dff64b568d59010db9830: I'm looking through these notes you found but there's nothing here.
